description: Creates a new Prime Contract for a given project.

params.name: number
params.description: The Prime Contract number. Defaults to the project numbering sequence for the respective contract type.

params.name: status
params.description: The Prime Contract status.

params.name: title
params.description: The Prime Contract title.

params.name: description
params.description: The Prime Contract description.

params.name: executed
params.description: The Prime Contract executed status.

params.name: vendor_id
params.description: The ID of the vendor for the contract. The selected invoice contacts must all belong to this vendor. When the vendor is changed, the invoice contacts are reset.

params.name: architect_id
params.description: The ID of the architect for the contract.

params.name: contractor_id
params.description: The ID of the contractor for the contract.

params.name: inclusions
params.description: Only applicable to Work Order Contracts. The inclusions of the Prime Contract.

params.name: exclusions
params.description: Only applicable to Work Order Contracts. The exclusions of the Prime Contract.

params.name: accounting_method
params.description: The accounting method for the contract. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: allow_comments
params.description: If true, comments are allowed on the Prime Contract; otherwise comments are not allowed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: auto_approve_prefilled_requisitions_on_payapp
params.description: If true, prefilled requisitions are auto-approved on payment applications; otherwise prefilled requisitions are not auto-approved. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: allow_markups
params.description: If true, markups are allowed on the Prime Contract; otherwise markups are not allowed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: change_order_level_of_detail
params.description: The level of details for showing change orders on invoices attached to this contract.

params.name: enable_ssov
params.description: If true, the subcontractor schedule of values (SSOV) is enabled for the Prime Contract; otherwise the SSOV is not enabled. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: allow_payment_applications
params.description: If true, invoices are allowed on the Prime Contract; otherwise invoices are not allowed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: allow_payments
params.description: If true, payments are allowed on the Prime Contract; otherwise payments are not allowed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: display_materials_retainage
params.description: If true, materials retainage is displayed on the Prime Contract; otherwise materials retainage is not displayed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: display_work_retainage
params.description: If true, work retainage is displayed on the Prime Contract; otherwise work retainage is not displayed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: show_cost_code_on_pdf
params.description: If true, cost codes are displayed on the Prime Contract PDF; otherwise cost codes are not displayed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: accessor_ids
params.description: Only applicable to private contracts. IDs of users in the project directory (see the Project Users endpoint). These users will be able to view the commitment contract.

params.name: private
params.description: If true, visible to admins and accessors only; otherwise visible to those with access to the Primes tool. Default based on project level setting.

params.name: show_line_items_to_non_admins
params.description: Only applicable to private contracts. If true, line items are visible to non-admins; otherwise visible to admins only.

params.description: Only applicable to Work Order Contracts.

description:
Returns all available custom field data types for a company, including their variants. This endpoint provides information about which data types are enabled for the company based on feature flags, the list of values (LOV) data types, and the available variants for each data type.

When `field_set_id` or `type` parameters are provided, the variants will be filtered to only include those supported by the specified field set.

params.name: company_id
params.description: Unique identifier for the company.

params.name: type
params.description: Configurable field set type to filter data type variants.  If provided, only variants supported by this field set type will be returned. For GenericToolItem, append the generic_tool_id (e.g., 'Conf...

params.name: field_set_id
params.description: ID of an existing configurable field set.  If provided, only variants supported by this specific field set will be returned. Takes priority over the 'type' parameter if both are provided.

summary: List coordination issues for a project (REST v2.0)
description:
Returns a paginated list of coordination issues visible to the user within the project.

Supports `view=ids_only` for `{ "data": [<id>, ...] }` (JSON numbers from `pluck(:id)`).

Query `included` controls which attributes are present on each item (see schema description).

When `save_sticky_filters` is truthy, list filters may be persisted for the tool (sticky filters).

params.name: company_id
params.description: Unique identifier for the company.

params.name: project_id
params.description: Unique identifier for the project.

params.name: view
params.description: When `ids_only`, response is id list only (no full blueprint objects).

params.name: save_sticky_filters
params.description: When true, persist current filters for the coordination issues tool where supported.

params.name: included
params.description:
Comma-separated blueprint field names (e.g. `assignee,title,uuid,location`). When omitted or empty,

summary: Create a Model Manager viewpoint and link it to the issue (REST v2.0)

Proxies to Model Manager `POST /rest/v2.0/companies/{company_id}/projects/{project_id}/bim/viewpoints`
(`RequestsCreateViewpointRequest`: `scene_id`, optional `name`, `payload` per MM OpenAPI), plus
**Procore-required** `bim_model_uuid` for application validation. Then inserts
`coordination_issue_bim_viewpoints` with `viewpoint_uuid` (MM viewpoint id).

Optional observer/notification side effects enqueue `CoordinationIssues::ObserverWorker`.

summary: Batch-get Model Manager viewpoints by UUID (REST v2.0, issue-scoped)
description:
Request body must include `ids` (non-empty array of UUID strings). The server **filters** `ids`
to those present on this issue as MM-backed mappings, preserves request order for the intersection,
then calls Model Manager `POST .../bim/viewpoints/batch`.

If no ids remain after filtering, returns `{ "data": [] }` **without** calling Model Manager.

Response body is forwarded from Model Manager (typically `{ "data": [ ... ] }`).

Resolves a join row on this issue: **`viewpoint_uuid`** match first, else legacy **`bim_viewpoint_id`**.

  **`primary`** and **`position`** merged from the join row (same idea as **PATCH** `200`).
- **Legacy:** `BimViewpointBlueprint` view `:procore_v0_1` for the linked `BimViewpoint`, plus **`primary`** /
  **`position`** from the join row.

row by **`viewpoint_uuid`** matching that id on this coordination issue (numeric `BimViewpoint` ids are
not valid on this path).

params.description: The Commitment Contract description.

params.description: The Commitment Contract executed status.

params.description: The ID of the vendor for the contract. The selected invoice contacts must all belong to this vendor. When the vendor is changed, the invoice contacts are reset.

params.name: assignee_id
params.description: Only applicable to Purchase Order Contracts. The ID of the user assigned to the Commitment Contract. The user must belong to the project directory (see the Project Users endpoint).

params.name: signature_required
params.description: If true, a signature is required to execute the contract; otherwise no signature is required.

params.description: The subcontractor schedule of values (SSOV) status. Only applicable to contracts which use an SSOV.

params.description: Only applicable to Purchase Order Contracts. The address to which invoices are sent. The default will be the bill to address of the most recently created purchase order.

params.name: ship_to_address
params.description: Only applicable to Purchase Order Contracts. The address to which materials are shipped. The default will be the ship to address of the most recently created purchase order.

params.description: Only applicable to Purchase Order Contracts. The shipping method for the Commitment Contract.

params.description: The accounting method for the contract. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: allow_comments
params.description: If true, comments are allowed on the Commitment Contract; otherwise comments are not allowed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: allow_markups
params.description: If true, markups are allowed on the Commitment Contract; otherwise markups are not allowed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: change_order_level_of_detail
params.description: The level of details for showing change orders on invoices attached to this contract.

params.description: If true, the subcontractor schedule of values (SSOV) is enabled for the Commitment Contract; otherwise the SSOV is not enabled. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: allow_change_orders_ssov
params.description: If true, the subcontractor schedule of values (SSOV) can be enabled on change orders for this Commitment Contract. When enabled, Change Order SSOV toggles become available. Default is false.

params.description: If true, invoices are allowed on the Commitment Contract; otherwise invoices are not allowed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.name: allow_payments
params.description: If true, payments are allowed on the Commitment Contract; otherwise payments are not allowed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.description: If true, materials retainage is displayed on the Commitment Contract; otherwise materials retainage is not displayed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.description: If true, work retainage is displayed on the Commitment Contract; otherwise work retainage is not displayed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.description: If true, cost codes are displayed on the Commitment Contract PDF; otherwise cost codes are not displayed. Default is driven by a project setting.

params.description: Only applicable to private contracts. If true, line items are visible to non-admins; otherwise visible to admins only.

params.name: filters[id][]
params.description:
Assign item(s) to the specified WBS Code IDs.

summary: Bulk Create Action Plans for Locations
description:
Creates multiple Action Plans from a single Action Plan Template at a specified location. The endpoint enqueues background jobs to create the specified number of Action Plans asynchronously.

The template must be in "published" status. Templates in "draft" or "in_revision" status cannot be used to create plans.

Each Action Plan will be created with all sections, items, assignees, references, and test record requests copied from the template.

params.description: The number of Action Plans to create (1-1000)

description:
Return a list of all Tax Types for a given Company

A Tax Type represents a "value added tax" or "goods and services tax"
which needs to be accounted for on a commitment or an invoice generated
by Procore. In many places there will just be a single tax to deal with,
for example in Australia it will be GST, in the UK it will be VAT.
Other countries may require that multiple taxes are displayed,
for example in Canada an invoice may be required to include both GST and PST.

params.name: filters[query]
params.description: Return item(s) containing search query

params.name: filters[received_from_id]
params.description: Filter results by received from id

params.name: filters[responsible_contractor_id]
params.description: Array of Responsible Contractor IDs. A single Responsible Contractor ID is also accepted.

params.name: filters[attachments]
params.description: Array of boolean values to filter submittals by "Attachments" status.

params.name: filters[specification_section_id]
params.description: Array of Specification Section IDs. A single Specification Section ID is also accepted.

params.name: filters[approver_id]
params.description: Array of User IDs. Return item(s) where the specified User IDs are in the approver list. A single integer is also accepted.

params.name: filters[cost_code_id]
params.description: Array of Cost Code IDs. A single Cost Code ID is also accepted.

params.name: filters[current_revision]
params.description: Default false. If true, only current revisions are shown. If false, all submittals are shown, regardless of whether or not it is the current revision.

params.description: Array of Divisions to filter on. A Division is the first two digits from the Specification Section Number. A single Division is also accepted.

params.name: filters[location_id]
params.description: Array of Location IDs. A single Location ID is also accepted.

params.name: filters[submittal_manager_id]
params.description: Array of Submittal Manager IDs. A single Submittal Manager ID is also accepted.

params.name: filters[number]
params.description: Array of Numbers. A single Number is also accepted.

params.name: filters[submittal_package_id]
params.description: Array of Submittal Package IDs. Returns item(s) associated with the specified Submittal Package IDs. A single integer value is also accepted.

params.name: filters[response_id]
params.description: Array of Response IDs. A single Response ID is also accepted.

params.name: filters[required_on_site_date]
params.description: Array of dates (date range). Returns item(s) with their required on site date withing the specified dates. A single date is also accepted. style: form

params.name: filters[status_id]
params.description: Array of Status IDs. A single Status ID is also accepted.

params.name: filters[type]
params.description: Array of Submittal Types. A single Submittal Type is also accepted.

params.name: filters[task_id]
params.description: Array of Submittal Task IDs. Returns item(s) associated with the specified Submittal Task IDs. A single integer value is also accepted.

params.name: filters[revision]
params.description: Array of Submittal Revision values. A single Submittal Revision is also accepted.

params.name: filters[specification_area_id]
params.description: Array of specification area IDs to filter submittals by. A single value is also accepted. Use "NULL" to filter submittals with no specification area.

params.name: filters[unpackaged]
params.description: Parseable to boolean value, filters out unpackaged Submittals.

params.name: filters[workflow_progress]
params.description: Array of strings to filter submittals by "Workflow Progress" status. Available options: on_track, off_track, overdue, paused, none

`YYY...

params.name: first_name
params.description: First Name of the Person.

params.name: last_name
params.description: Last Name of the Person.

params.name: is_user
params.description: Determines if the Person can log into the app.

params.name: is_assignable
params.description: Determines if the Person can be assigned to tasks.

params.name: status
params.description: The status of the Person. `active` means the person is visible in all pages, while `inactive` hides the person unless filtered. Inactive People do not count against billing plans.

params.name: group_ids
params.description: Array of UUIDs representing the Groups this Person belongs to. Can be empty if the Person is an Admin.

params.name: email
params.description: The email that the Person will log in with. Required if `is_user` is true.

params.name: permission_level_id
params.description: UUID of the Permission Level assigned to the Person.

params.name: no_invite
params.description: If `true`, the Person will be created with all user properties but will not receive an invite until triggered manually by an Admin.

params.name: password
params.description: Password for logging in. If not provided, an email will be sent to the user to set their password. Must be at least 8 characters with one uppercase, one lowercase, and one number.

params.name: notification_profile_id
params.description: UUID of the Notification Profile for the user.

params.name: phone
params.description: The Person's phone number, including country and area code. Must be unique among all registered People. **Note:** Pass `null` or exclude the field if the Person should not have a phone number.

params.name: can_receive_sms
params.description: Determines if the Person can receive SMS notifications.

params.name: can_receive_email
params.description: Determines if the Person can receive email notifications.

params.name: can_receive_mobile
params.description: Determines if the Person can receive mobile push notifications if they have the app installed.

params.name: address_1
params.description: First part of the Person's address.

params.name: address_2
params.description: Second part of the Person's address (e.g., Apartment, Suite, Unit).

params.name: city_town
params.description: The city or town where the Person is located.

params.name: state_province
params.description: The state or province where the Person is located.

params.name: zipcode
params.description: The postal/zip code of the Person.

description:
Creating an Upload is the first step in associating a file to a resource
in Procore. Creating an Upload can be seen as fetching instruction on how
to post your file directly to Procore's storage service.

The instructions contain three properties: a UUID to reference the
Upload, a URL which has to be used to post the file, and fields which
need to be posted together with the file.

To upload the file you must POST to the URL in the _url_ property with
a multipart/form-data body (see RFC 2388)...

storage service and without the filename they will save on t...

value is less important because HTTP clients and operat...

description:
Update the specified User.

#### Uploading avatar
To upload avatar you must upload whole payload as `multipart/form-data` content-type and
specify each parameter as form-data together with `user[avatar]` as file.

#### Country and State codes
The `country_code` and `state_code` parameter values must conform to the ISO-3166 Alpha-2 specification.
See [Working with Country Codes](/documentation/country-codes) for additional information.

description:
Return a list of all Users associated with a Company.

See [Filtering on List Actions](https://developers.procore.com/documentation/filtering-on-list-actions) for information on using the filtering capabilities provided by this endpoint.

params.name: company_id
params.description: Unique identifier for the company.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number for paginated results (default: 1)

params.name: per_page
params.description: Number of items per page (default: 100, max: 100)

params.name: filters[active]
params.description: If true, returns item(s) with a status of 'active'.

params.name: filters[created_at]
params.description:
Return item(s) created within the specified ISO 8601 datetime range.
Formats:
`YYYY-MM-DD`...`YYYY-MM-DD` - Date
`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`...`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ` - DateTime with UTC Offset
`YYYY-MM-...

params.name: filters[updated_at]
params.description:
Return item(s) last updated within the specified ISO 8601 datetime range.
Formats:
`YYYY-MM-DD`...`YYYY-MM-DD` - Date
`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`...`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ` - DateTime with UTC Offset
`YYY...

params.name: filters[vendor_id]
params.description: Return item(s) with the specified Vendor IDs.

params.name: filters[origin_id]
params.description: Origin ID. Returns item(s) with the specified Origin ID.

params.name: filters[trade_id][]
params.description: Returns users whose vendor record is associated with the specified trade id(s).

params.name: filters[search]
params.description: Return users where the search string matches the user's first name, last name, email address, keywords, job title, or company name

params.name: sort
params.description: Return items with the specified sort.

summary: Bulk add company users to projects
description:
This endpoint allows company users to be bulk added to multiple projects with a permission template in one request.
If the user was already part of that project it will not update the assigned permission template.

params.name: company_id
params.description: Unique identifier for the company.

params.name: users
params.description: Array of existing company users with a permission template id and project id that they will be assinged to. The maximum amount per request is 1000 user_id, project_id, permission_template_id triple...

summary: Bulk remove company users from projects
description: This endpoint allows company users to be bulk removed from multiple projects.

The visibility of Change Order Packages, Potential Change Orders & Change Order Requests

2-tier: Change Order Packages, Potential Change Orders

params.description: If true, will include WBS Codes with division segment items. Default is true.

params.description: The required segments for this Work Breakdown Structure operation

params.name: filters[status][]
params.description: Filter results to only return codes with the included statuses. Options are 'active' or 'inactive'. Defaults to returning all results.

params.name: filters[updated_at]
params.description: Filter results to only return codes that were updated within the range of the two specified ISO 8601 timestamps separated by the ... delimiter.

params.name: scope
params.description: Filter results to only return codes that match the specified WBS scope.

params.name: query
params.description: Searches the WBS code and description values and returns results sorted in descending order of relevance to the search query.

params.name: group_type
params.description: Along with 'group_id', groups WBS codes by the specified group type and group ID. Only supported option is 'contract'.

params.name: group_id
params.description: Along with 'group_type', groups WBS codes by the specified group type and group ID. Only supported option is a contract ID.

params.name: hide_not_in_group
params.description: If true, will hide WBS codes that are not in the specified 'group_type' and 'group_id'. Default is true. If false, WBS codes in the specified group will be returned first followed by WBS codes not ...

params.name: page
params.description: Page number for paginated results (default: 1)

params.name: per_page
params.description: Number of items per page (default: 100, max: 100)
